creator: rodrigo@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hi Keyue,

As kurt said, there is some issues with the packaging steps, as an example this is the poudriere output for the qt5 flavor:

```
=======================<phase: package        >============================
===== env: 'PKG_NOTES=build_timestamp built_by' 'PKG_NOTE_build_timestamp=2024-12-18T11:54:01+0000' 'PKG_NOTE_built_by=poudriere-git-3.4.1_1' NO_DEPENDS=yes USER=root UID=0 GID=0
===>  Building packages for yass-qt5-1.16.2
===>   Building yass-qt5-1.16.2
pkg-static: Unable to access file /wrkdirs/usr/ports/net/yass/work-qt5/stage/usr/local/share/locale/en/LC_MESSAGES/yass.mo:No such file or directory
[...]
pkg-static: Unable to access file /wrkdirs/usr/ports/net/yass/work-qt5/stage/usr/local/share/icons/hicolor/512x512/apps/io.github.chilledheart.yass.png:No such file or directory
```

I don't know how you test your port, but I encourage you to install poudriere, and use the interactive mode to help you with the debugging
https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/handbook/book/#ports-poudriere
(Look at the -b switch in man poudriere-bulk to save time ;) )

Also you must use portlint, portfmt and portclippy linters to ensure you port matches the ports requirements
https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/porters-handbook/book/#testing-portclippy

Feel free to ask help if you need guidance in how to fix a specific issue.

All the best
-- Rodrigo
